sourceTitle: The Other Side of the River, The Other Side of the Sea

modules.exactText: This is the seder’s fulcrum, the turning point that leverages our collective memories of slavery and turns them into collective obligation.

locator.edition: The Other Side of the River, The Other Side of the Sea
locator.sourceLocation: PDF sheet p. 6 (printed p. 15), In Every Generation

conditions: Review embedded artwork and quotations item by item

modules.exactText: Lift up the tomato on the seder plate and say: “This tomato on our seder plate: What does it mean?” It represents the workers who picked it and the organizing that has changed their lives.

locator.edition: The Other Side of the River, The Other Side of the Sea
locator.sourceLocation: PDF sheet p. 4 (printed p. 41), Rabban Gamliel Says
